dimensions.rationale: All twenty-six frozen S2 requirements are explicit, required and reciprocally projected exactly once into required workstreams, acceptance criteria and thirty-six evidence artifacts; the final severity and product-budget gaps from r01 are resolved.

Core lifecycle semantics, thin skills, isolated surface adapters, change-set providers, authority profiles and release tooling have explicit one-way boundaries, fail-closed behavior and compatibility seams.

Canonical workstream ownership is disjoint and its DAG is acyclic, but every worker entry also declares the forbidden dependsOn field, duplicating DAG authority and making the schema-3.0 ready profile fail.

The required adapter path is now fully offline, pinned to the immutable contract inventory and capped honestly at EXPERIMENTAL; optional live promotion cannot block or weaken the required release DAG.
Bootstrap, per-launch, post-attempt and release neutrality scopes are fail-closed, externally trusted, signed, subject-bound and redacted, with explicit secret and deny-authority boundaries.
The frozen product profile defines exact S0/S1/S2 logical-call, token, context, tool, evidence, parallelism and wall-time ceilings plus deterministic p50/p95 benchmark and live-calibration gates.

Each requirement has reciprocal acceptance and bounded evidence with a producer command, receipt, validator identity, runtime class, threshold, freshness and reuse rule; task and final acceptance reject every open Medium, High or Blocker finding.

Offline adapter execution is non-blocking, while externally owned neutrality, live-calibration and exact nine-leg CI authorities have explicit launch gates, signed receipts, timeouts and fail-closed aggregation.

The specification lineage, plan-review disclosure, approvals, bounded retries, durable state, neutrality gates and no-open-Medium verdicts are explicit, but the freeze candidate cannot pass deterministic ready validation while worker.dependsOn remains present.

The provider-neutral fifteen-task DAG retains eight effective waves, width four, zero overlaps and zero duplicate validation commands; maximum task context is 52565 bytes under the frozen 65536-byte ceiling, with task-local progressive disclosure.

findings.message: Schema 3.0 makes workstreams the only canonical dependency DAG, but all fifteen worker entries declare dependsOn: fourteen repeat non-empty worker dependencies and neutrality-worker carries the forbidden empty field. The deterministic plan-ready validator therefore stops with 'worker.dependsOn is forbidden; workstreams are the canonical DAG'. Even where the duplicated values currently match, the second authority is non-conformant and can drift during packet compilation or execution.
findings.requiredAction: Remove dependsOn from every worker entry while preserving the dependency graph exclusively in workstreams[].dependsOn. Re-run the ready-profile package validator and quality analyzer against the corrected subject, then submit the new subject for independent review before freeze.

findings.message: Revision 2 adds the complete bootstrap negative suite, a full repository scan, signed controller-owned pre-launch and post-attempt neutrality gates for WS-02 through WS-15, explicit external trust inputs, full-history and recursive release-payload evidence, and final verification of signer, subject, scope, freshness and zero-result counters.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for PLAN-002; preserve the frozen neutrality authority profile, controller receipt bindings, full bootstrap/release scopes and redaction boundary when correcting PLAN-007.

findings.message: Frozen specification revision 3, its reciprocal manifest projections and every task/final gate now require no unresolved Medium, High or Blocker finding for ACCEPTED or COMPLETE, with Blocker stopping execution immediately.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for PLAN-003; retain the exact task and final severity thresholds during the manifest-only correction.

findings.message: The exact first specification-review subject is preserved as product-specification-r01-round01.snapshot.json, specification-review-r02 links specification-review-r01 by exact content identity, and the append-only r03/r04 review chain reaches the valid specification-freeze-r03 receipt. The unavailable rejected plan-r01 subject is separately disclosed without fabricating replay, while this full r02 review links the exact r01 review artifact.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for PLAN-001; preserve the specification chain, plan-review-r01 artifact and plan-review-r01-subject-disclosure.json unchanged.

findings.message: WS-10 through WS-13 now run with network denied and no live-surface capability gate, consume the immutable normalized contract inventory, pin closed offline compatibility descriptors and emit release-eligible EXPERIMENTAL evidence; VERIFIED remains an optional separately authorized live promotion.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for PLAN-004; keep live surface capability outside the required DAG and prevent offline evidence from promoting maturity above EXPERIMENTAL.

findings.message: The immutable product budget profile now freezes S0/S1/S2 hard ceilings, stage token limits, logical-call and cumulative-context semantics, five benchmark scenarios, two hundred deterministic cold/warm runs, absolute p95 SLOs, later-release p50/p95 regression limits, signed baseline sequencing and six bounded live calibration runs.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for PLAN-005; preserve the exact profile, benchmark authority, reciprocal EV-RESOURCE and EV-COST-BENCHMARK contracts, and distinction between implementation-run orchestration limits and shipped product limits.

findings.message: Revision 2 freezes the exact Ubuntu, macOS and Windows by CPython 3.11 through 3.13 matrix, nine distinct CI-owned evidence and receipt paths, an external signed allowlist authority, exact per-leg environment identities and a signed fail-closed aggregate consumed by release evidence.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for PLAN-006; preserve all nine leg identities, per-leg receipts, external authority and exact aggregate consumption during the manifest-only correction.

summary: CHANGES_REQUIRED after a full independent ten-dimension review of plan revision 2. The reviewed subject is bound to 730ca7e6c5841475c0aaf8dc44aeb706df5f49bbd9b6d3ee1755b98da5a1bd82 and the exact r01 predecessor review is bound to e1abab9c75105853d1064f15c602b8594dc25bb2c05bb2b0303e29dbe9ffce0b; the missing rejected-r01 subject remains explicitly disclosed rather than reconstructed. PLAN-001 through PLAN-006 are resolved, the frozen S2 specification chain is valid, semantic traceability covers twenty-six requirements and thirty-six evidence artifacts, and the DAG has fifteen tasks, eight effective waves, width four, zero write overlaps, zero open questions and zero duplicate commands. One new HIGH finding remains: PLAN-007. The ready-profile validator rejects worker.dependsOn because workstreams are the canonical DAG, so this plan cannot be frozen until those fifteen forbidden fields are removed and the corrected subject is independently re-reviewed.
optimizationNotes: Compared with r01, critical path, effective waves and maximum width remain 8, 8 and 4, with zero write overlaps and zero duplicate commands; the safe dependency shape therefore needs no redesign.

Evidence count increased from 20 to 36, validation invocations from 20 to 36 and minimum tool calls from 59 to 75 to close the neutrality, resource and platform-matrix proof gaps. Referenced context increased from 231681 to 420640 bytes, but the maximum task packet remains 52565 bytes under the 65536-byte ceiling.
Remove only the forbidden worker.dependsOn members. Keeping the workstream DAG unchanged resolves PLAN-007 without changing waves, ownership, acceptance, evidence coverage or context projections.

Retain the single controller-owned content-addressed neutrality gate and referenced authority profile; duplicating its contract or external values into task packets would increase context and weaken the redaction boundary.

The r01 subject disclosure remains authoritative: do not spend review or tool budget attempting to reconstruct the unavailable rejected draft. Continue through exact review-artifact lineage and a full audit of each new subject.
